title: Nonwoven substrate and method for its manufacture
abstract: This invention relates to a nonwoven substrate, containing lyocell fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex, viscose fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex and cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section. It further relates to a method of manufacturing such nonwovens as well as to their use and to wet wipes containing them.
claims: Claims Claims 1. Nonwoven substrate, containing lyocell fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex, viscose fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex and cellulosic man made fibers with a stiffening cross-section and with a cut length of between 30 and 70 mm. 2. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 1 , wherein the cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section show a round cross-section and a titer of between 5.5 and 7.5 dtex. 3. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 2, containing 35% to 45% lyocell fibers with a cut length of between 28mm and 48mm, 35% to 45% viscose fibers with a cut length of between 30mm and 50mm and 15% to 25% cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section with a cut length of between 50mm and 70mm. 4. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 2, wherein the cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section are lyocell fibers. 5. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 1 , wherein the cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section show a multilobal cross-section and a titer of between 2.5 and 4.5 dtex. 6. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 5, containing 10% to 20% lyocell fibers with a cut length of between 28mm and 48mm, 65% to 75% viscose fibers with a cut length of between 30mm and 50mm and 10% to 20% cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section with a cut length of between 30mm and 50mm. 7. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 5, wherein the cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section are viscose fibers. 8. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 1 , which shows a CD (Cross direction) tensile strength of at least 17N/5cm, preferably at least 20N/5cm, in dry stage before converting. 9. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 1 , which shows a lotion consumption of less than 5 ml/wipe. 10. Nonwoven substrate according to claim 1 , which shows an elongation of less than 25%/5cm in MD (machine direction) and less than 130%/5cm in CD (Cross direction). 11. Method for the manufacture of a nonwoven substrate, comprising the following sequence of steps in an inline process: a. Providing lyocell fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex, viscose fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex and cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross-section with a cut length of between 30 and 70 mm, b. opening and mixing all 3 fiber types of step a. until getting a homogeneous fiber fleece , c. carding the fiber fleece through a conventional commercial carding equipment, d. condensing the carded fiber fleece into a 30 to 70 g/m 2 XYZ orientated fiber fleece (dry nonwoven web, 2 to 6 layers), e. conveying this dry nonwoven web into a spunlacing unit f. conveying the nonwoven web through a dewatering unit and g. through a drying unit to removing moisture until natural moisture level of the fibers (10% +/-1%), h. winding the nonwoven web into a âmother rollâ form, characterized in that in the spunlacing unit (step e.) Z consolidation and nonwoven surface embossing are performed by 4 or more high pressure jet beams. 12. Use of the nonwoven substrate of claim 1 for the manufacture of wipes, in particular wet wipes packed in cross-folded wipe packs, showing a wipe dispensing of less than 2 missed dispensings per 22 wipes. 13. Wipes, showing a wipe dispensing of less than 2 missed dispensings per 22 wipes, characterized in that they are containing a nonwoven substrate, containing lyocell fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex, viscose fibers with a titer of between 1.5 and 1.9 dtex and cellulosic man-made fibers with a stiffening cross- section and with a cut length of between 30 and 70 mm.
